It's the only shoe in Andrea Boldrini's range fitted with a Vibram Grip outsole - a soft, high-friction compound that bites on steep limestone pockets and granite crystals alike. X-tension rubber wraps over the toe for hooking power and lateral support, while a zig-zag velcro strap locks the foot in place for quick transitions between burns. The 100% microfiber upper resists stretch, so the fit stays precise over time. Handmade in France.

belay_devices.description: Beal V-Groove friction system provides controlled braking across a wide range of rope diameters. The straight edge decreases rope wear, and each device has an individual serial number for traceability.
pros: V-Groove friction adapts to rope diameter

belay_devices.description: The Air Force 3 adds guide mode to Beal V-Groove design, allowing belaying one or two followers from above. A small release hole makes it easy to lower under tension. Optimized for modern thin ropes.
cons: Less widely available than Reverso or ATC Guide

belay_devices.description: Beal's assisted-braking device with anti-panic function. The Birdie offers smooth lowering and reliable braking in a design that's easier to learn than the GriGri for some climbers.

The original Air Traffic Controller — the device that defined the modern tube belay device category. Simple, lightweight, and reliable with a wide rope range. No frills, no guide mode, just a proven design that has been trusted by millions of climbers since 1993.

A thin-rope specialist tube device designed for alpine climbing with half and twin ropes. At just 74g, the ATC-Alpine Guide is one of the lightest belay devices with guide mode, making it ideal for weight-conscious alpinists. Handles ropes as thin as 6.9mm.

The most versatile belay device available. The ATC Guide does everything — lead belay, top belay, guide mode for belaying a second from above, single and double rope rappel. Its simplicity is its strength: no moving parts, works with virtually any rope, and weighs under 90g. The go-to device for multi-pitch climbing, trad, and alpine where versatility trumps assisted braking.
cons: No assisted braking when lead belaying
Requires constant vigilance on brake hand
Lowering in guide mode can be tricky
Less comfortable for long projecting sessions

belay_devices.description: A single-rope passive assisted braking device that uses geometry to pinch the rope between device and carabiner during a fall. Ultra-lightweight at 94g, the ATC-Pilot offers brake assistance in a familiar tube-style package — perfect for gym and single-pitch cragging.
cons: Less braking power than cam devices

belay_devices.description: The enhanced version of the classic ATC with grooved friction channels on one side for extra braking power. Flip it over for a smoother, lower-friction feed when conditions require it. No guide mode, so it's not ideal as a primary multi-pitch device, but its simplicity, double rope compatibility, and unbeatable price make it a perfect starter device or reliable backup.

The Black Diamond Super 8 is a modern figure-eight style device with ears to prevent girth hitching. Dual friction modes and compatibility with ropes from 8.1–11mm make it versatile for rappelling and rescue.
pros: Great for SAR and rescue teams
cons: Less friction control than tubes for lead belaying

belay_devices.description: CAMP's contender against the GriGri, with an anti-panic feature baked in. The Matik uses a cam mechanism similar to the GriGri but adds a spring-loaded anti-panic handle that re-engages the cam if the lever is opened too far — similar to the GriGri+ but in a different form factor. Lighter than the GriGri+ and cheaper, making it an attractive alternative for climbers wanting assisted braking with extra safety.
pros: Anti-panic at a lower price than GriGri+
cons: Less smooth slack feeding than GriGri
No guide mode or double rope rappel

belay_devices.description: The CAMP Shell is an ultralight guide-mode belay device at just 60g. Unique shell-shaped design with asymmetric V-grooves provides different friction levels for lead vs top-rope belaying. Guide mode allows belaying one or two seconds from an anchor.

The Swiss Army knife of belay devices. The Alpine Up offers assisted braking in lead mode, guide mode for multi-pitch, and standard rappel — all in a 160g package. One of the most versatile devices on the market.
pros: Works with single and half ropes
cons: Braking not as positive as cam devices
Can be fiddly with thin ropes

belay_devices.description: A GriGri alternative with a twist: the Click Up's anti-panic function locks the rope if the handle is pulled too hard. Great for nervous beginners and gym climbing.

The upgraded Click Up+ adds an anti-panic system to the popular Click Up design. Uses a special carabiner (included) that enables the braking geometry. If the belayer panics and pulls the handle too far, the device re-engages the brake automatically.

One of the lightest tube-style belay devices on the market at just 45g. The Mantis is DMM's minimalist tube device — simple, reliable, and extremely lightweight. Ideal as a backup device or for weight-conscious climbers.

DMM's answer to the lowering-in-guide-mode problem. The Pivot features a unique pivot mechanism that makes lowering a second in guide mode dramatically easier than competing devices. Where the ATC Guide and Reverso require workarounds to release an auto-blocked climber, the Pivot's design allows a controlled lower with a simple lever motion. A favorite among UK trad climbers.
pros: Unique pivot mechanism for smooth release

belay_devices.description: The Giga Jul extends the Mega Jul concept with wider rope compatibility down to 7.1mm twins and a switchable assisted/manual braking mode. The toggle lets you choose between assisted braking for lead belaying and free-running mode for smoother rope feeding. Accepts the widest rope diameter range of any brake-assist device, making it uniquely suited for teams using half or twin ropes on alpine routes.
pros: Handles twin ropes better than any rival
cons: More friction in auto-block than competitors
Learning curve steeper than single-mode devices

belay_devices.description: Edelrid's entry-level passive assisted braking device with geometry that blocks the rope during a fall. Easier rope payout than the Mega Jul thanks to optimized slot geometry. A great choice for gym climbing and indoor/outdoor single-pitch use.
pros: Easy rope payout for lead belaying

belay_devices.description: The Swiss Army knife of belay devices. The Mega Jul combines passive assisted braking with guide mode and double rope capability in an ultralight 65g steel package — something no other device achieves. Thinner than a phone and weighing 61% less than a GriGri, it handles everything from lead belaying to double-rope rappelling. The tradeoff: a steep learning curve and technique-dependent braking.
pros: Lightest assisted braking with guide mode
Full rope type compatibility with brake assist

belay_devices.description: The Edelrid Micro Jul is a tiny stainless-steel passive assisted-braking device designed for thin ropes from 6.9–8.9mm. Originally created to pair with the groundbreaking 6.9mm Flycatcher rope, it features guide mode for belaying seconds and can also be used for rappelling. The assisted braking engages through device geometry — no moving parts.
cons: Not for thick single ropes above 8.9mm
Requires specific technique for smooth feeding
Steep learning curve compared to tubes

belay_devices.description: The Edelrid Nano Jul is a stainless-steel tube-style guide device optimized for thin half and twin ropes from 7.1–8.9mm. Its guide mode allows belaying one or two followers from above on multi-pitch routes. Built for durability with no moving parts.

Edelrid's newest cam-assisted device with a revolutionary closure system that clips directly to the belay loop — no separate carabiner needed. This eliminates the risk of cross-loading or carabiner twist during falls. Features anti-panic on the lowering handle and a clean industrial design. Positioned as a direct GriGri competitor with the added safety of integrated attachment.
pros: Integrated belay loop attachment — no carabiner needed
cons: New design — limited field testing data

belay_devices.description: Grivel lightest tube belay device at just 64g. A simple, no-frills design with V-grooves for controlled braking on a wide range of rope diameters from 7.3–11mm.

The Grivel Master Pro is a lightweight guide-mode belay device with cooling holes for heat dissipation and horns for extra friction with thin ropes. Works as a self-locking plate for belaying seconds from above.
cons: Plain aluminum wears faster than steel

belay_devices.description: The Grivel Scream is an ultralight specialist tube device designed exclusively for thin ropes from 5–8mm. Perfect for alpine and ice climbing where every gram counts and half/twin ropes are standard.
pros: Great for ice and mixed climbing

belay_devices.description: Designed for mountain guides, the Grivel Shuttle features an optimized guide-mode geometry that makes pulling up rope easier on multi-pitch routes, saving energy for the actual climbing.
pros: Easy rope pulling in guide mode
cons: Slightly heavy at 85g for a tube device

belay_devices.description: A budget-friendly Italian-made plate device with guide mode capability. The Gi-Gi handles ropes from 8mm all the way up to 13mm, making it one of the most accommodating belay devices. Perfect as a lightweight guide-mode device at an unbeatable price.

A compact and lightweight active assisted braking device that rivals the GriGri at a lower price. The Lifeguard uses a cam mechanism for reliable brake assistance. Hot forged aircraft-grade aluminum and stainless steel construction ensures durability.
cons: Handle can override cam in full throttle

belay_devices.description: The Safeguard is the springless version of Mad Rock Lifeguard. Without the internal spring, the cam is always on — any force from the climber side blocks the rope. Designed primarily for rescue and rigging rather than lead climbing.
Mammut's ultralight tube device with guide mode. At just 57g it's perfect for weight-conscious alpinists who need a reliable device for half and twin ropes on long routes.

Mammut's innovative and robust tube-style belay device with V-shaped grooves and abrasion-resistant steel inserts. At just 80g, it allows guide-mode belaying of one or two seconds independently. The steel insert prevents overheating during long rappels.
pros: Steel inserts prevent wear and overheating
cons: Steel adds slight weight vs pure aluminum

belay_devices.description: Mammut's passive assisted braking device — a lightweight alternative to mechanical cam devices. The Smart 2.0 uses a simple pinching mechanism between the device body and carabiner to provide braking assistance during falls. Easy to learn, lightweight at 75g, and significantly cheaper than the GriGri. Ideal for gym climbers and beginners who want extra security without the complexity of a cam device.
cons: Single rope only, no guide mode
Higher friction in guide mode testing

belay_devices.description: The alpine version of Mammut's popular Smart 2.0, adding guide mode and two rope slots for multi-pitch climbing with half ropes. Combines passive brake assistance with tube-device versatility. Handles ropes from 7.5mm for alpine and ice climbing.
pros: Wide rope range for alpine use
cons: Assisted braking not as strong as cam

belay_devices.description: The Metolius BRD (Belay/Rappel Device) is a CNC-machined tubular device with engineered friction channels and cooling fins for heat dissipation during long lowers and rappels. A friction horn on the cable end provides extra stopping power when needed.
pros: Extra friction horn for controlled lowers

belay_devices.description: A Czech-made passive-assisted tube device from Ocun with guide mode. The Hawk uses geometry to provide assisted braking while maintaining tube-device versatility. Very light at 72g with guide mode — a great all-rounder for multi-pitch climbing.
cons: Assisted braking less powerful than cam devices

belay_devices.description: The gold standard of assisted braking belay devices. The GriGri's cam-assisted blocking provides reliable fall arrest while maintaining smooth rope feeding for experienced belayers. Updated version handles ropes down to 8.5mm with improved cam engagement and 3:1 mechanical advantage for comfortable lowering. The most popular belay device at any crag worldwide.
pros: Excellent for projecting and long belays
cons: Single rope only — no rappel on doubled rope
Can lock up when feeding slack quickly

belay_devices.description: The safety-enhanced version of the GriGri with additional features for guides and newer climbers. Anti-panic handle automatically re-engages the cam if the lever is opened too far during lowering. Lead/top-rope toggle adjusts cam spring tension — tighter in top-rope mode for extra security. 3:1 mechanical advantage makes it ideal for weight mismatches and big wall hauling.

The next evolution in assisted braking. The Neox features an integrated wheel that eliminates the biggest complaint about the GriGri — premature cam engagement when feeding slack. The wheel spins freely during normal rope feeding but stops and engages the cam during falls. Universally praised as a game-changer for lead belaying, offering ATC-smooth slack feeding with GriGri-level fall arrest.
cons: Newer design — less field track record
Single rope only — no double strand rappel

belay_devices.description: Petzl's ultralight tube device with guide mode. At just 59g, the Reverso is one of the lightest full-featured tube devices available, making it the alpinist's choice. Works with single, half, and twin ropes for complete versatility. Smooth rappelling characteristics make it a favorite for long multi-pitch descents. The asymmetric shape provides two friction levels depending on orientation.
cons: Lowering in guide mode requires technique
Thin walls wear faster on sharp edges
Less braking friction than ATC Guide

belay_devices.description: Petzl's ultralight and compact tubular belay device — the lightest in the Petzl belay lineup at just 55g. A simple, reliable tube device for gym and outdoor climbing. Handles all rope types from 6.9mm to 11mm, making it versatile for any climbing style.
cons: Requires solid technique for lead belay

belay_devices.description: Salewa's ergonomic assisted-braking device. The Ergo's cam mechanism provides reliable braking while the handle design keeps your hand in the correct position. A good alternative for Salewa loyalists.
cons: Brand less established in belay devices

belay_devices.description: The Singing Rock Buddy is an ultra-lightweight tube belay device at just 54g. Smooth rope handling and effective heat dissipation make it a great budget option for gym and crag climbing.
pros: Only 54g — one of the lightest tubes

belay_devices.description: The Singing Rock Shuttle features guide mode with self-locking function for belaying seconds. V-grooves provide high friction, and an eye-hole allows releasing the self-lock under tension. Can simultaneously belay two climbers.
The Trango Aries is a hot-forged 7075 aluminum guide-mode device with dual V channels for controlled braking. Wide rope range from 7.5–11mm makes it versatile for all climbing styles.
cons: Less well-known than Reverso or ATC Guide

belay_devices.description: The Trango Cinch is a cam-assisted belay device with a straightforward design for single ropes. Its spring-loaded cam locks automatically when loaded, and the smooth lowering lever provides controlled descents. Best suited for gym and sport climbing with thicker ropes.
A time-honored tube design with a pivoting center pin that allows flipping for customizable friction. V-shaped rope channels and tapered geometry provide versatile braking control.
cons: Narrower rope range than modern tubes

belay_devices.description: Trango's GriGri competitor with anti-panic and lead/TR mode switch. The Vergo's dual-mode design lets you optimize braking for lead or top-rope. A strong choice for gym and sport climbing.

A lightweight tube device with guide mode from Wild Country. At 77g, the Pro Guide Lite is one of the lightest devices with auto-block functionality for multi-pitch climbing. Excellent value for a versatile, no-frills belay/rappel device.
The panic-proof belay device. The Revo's bi-directional locking mechanism stops the rope in ANY scenario — whether you grip too hard or let go entirely. The safest assisted device on the market.
pros: Bi-directional locking — works if you grip or release

ropes.description: This low stretch type A rope with a diameter of 10.5mm is extremely strong (46 daN/g). This ACCESS UC rope is made in the same way is our flapship product for working at height INDUSTRIE 11 and is therefore ideally suited for all risky operations.
pros: Low stretch for precise rappel and rescue work
cons: Keep it physically separated from lead ropes in storage

ropes.description: ANTIDOTE 10.2 MM is a multipurpose rope especially designed for crag climbers. Its large diameter provides extra comfort when belaying and better resistance to abrasion. This model is also easy to handle and compact for traditional climbing.
pros: Water rolls off the treated outer layer
cons: Not the best choice when weight matters

ropes.description: Meet the toughest of the tough among traditional ropes. The APOLLO II rope with its 11mm diameter is made of a sheath capable of withstanding all the stresses of Big Wall climbing. It has an exceptional resistance to edges as well as a great safety reserve in intensive use. Designed to cope with all conditions, it will
pros: Exceptionally high fall rating for long-term durability
cons: Not the best choice when weight matters

ropes.description: Beal's Berlin 9.8 Unicore is a premium all-purpose rope combining the durability of Unicore construction with exceptional handling. Golden Dry treatment ensures reliability in all conditions.
pros: Fall rating well above average for the diameter

ropes.description: This thin rope brings together a wide range of qualities that make it highly satisfying to use. Compact, smooth, and ultra-light, it slips through any terrain with ease. With low impact force and designed to withstand a high number of falls, the BOOSTER III will soon become your best partner on the wall. Recommended fo
pros: Low impact force makes falls less jarring

ropes.description: Cette corde fine compile un grand nombre de qualités apportant une grande satisfaction lors de son utilisation. À la fois compacte, fluide et ultra légère, cette corde se faufile partout. Avec une force de choc basse et conçue pour supporter un nombre de chutes élevée, la corde BOOSTER III deviendra bientôt votre meill
pros: Low impact force makes falls less jarring
cons: Heavier pull through alpine terrain with ledges and corners

ropes.description: Beal's Cobra II 8.6 Unicore is a half rope designed for multipitch sport and alpine climbing. Unicore construction combined with Dry Cover treatment ensures maximum durability and reliability.
pros: Pleasant to handle on long belay sessions
cons: Must be used as half rope
Premium pricing for the features offered

ropes.description: The DIABLO 10.2 MM UNICORE rope is the first outdoor rope to be equipped with the UNICORE innovative technology that provides increased resistance and safety for climbers. It offers a reassuring diameter that is suitable for all climbers and will appeal to most climbers. A rope that remains compact while its smooth she
pros: Soft and flexible in the hand
Unicore tech for added safety if the sheath gets damaged
cons: Impact force on the high side for its class

ropes.description: The DIABLO 9.8MM rope is an improved and thinner version of its big sister the DIABLO 10.2. With its new very thin sheath, it is even more efficient when taking in slack and carabiners, and better than the more traditional smaller diameter ropes, with a lower weight. Its resistance to abrasion has also been improved. I
pros: Sheath doesn't soak up moisture like untreated alternatives
Unicore tech for added safety if the sheath gets damaged
Made by a company with decades of rope-making experience
cons: Replace sooner due to fewer rated falls

ropes.description: With its modern design incorporating the latest BÉAL innovations, the FLYER 10.2MM rope offers a good grip and relatively light weight while providing increased resistance to abrasion and moisture. The FLYER 10.2MM is positioned as an intermediate model between the TIGER and TOP GUN, allowing climbers to gradually get
pros: Low impact force makes falls less jarring
Comes from a brand with proven quality control
Suitable for its intended use case
cons: Not the best choice when weight matters

ropes.description: The ultralight twin rope for committed alpine objectives. Both strands clip into the same gear, providing the lightest system weight possible. Unicore bonding ensures safety if the sheath is damaged. Golden Dry offers maximum water protection for ice and mixed routes. Expert-only.
pros: Lightest twin system at 36g/m per strand
Exceptionally high fall rating for long-term durability

ropes.description: Beal's workhorse half rope for ice and alpine climbing. With an incredible 12 UIAA falls and Unicore bonding, it's one of the most durable half ropes you can buy. Golden Dry for wet conditions.
pros: Exceptionally high fall rating for long-term durability

ropes.description: Heavy-duty static rope for professional and industrial use.
pros: Low stretch for precise rappel and rescue work
Reliable for top-rope setups and fixed lines
Treatment blocks moisture and grit from getting into the core
cons: Never use for lead climbing - zero dynamic elongation

ropes.description: Ultra-low-stretch static rope for rescue and intervention work.
pros: Doesn't gain weight from moisture like untreated ropes
cons: Label or color-code carefully to prevent dangerous mix-ups

ropes.description: The JOKER 9.1MM rope is a single, double and twin rope that can be adapted to many different climbing situations. It offers the right balance between lightness, fluidity and safety to please experienced climbers and will also suit, thanks to its versatility, more traditional climbers looking for a rope that can be used
pros: Easy to carry on long multi-pitch days
Soft catches that are easier on the climber and gear
cons: Thin diameter needs careful device selection

ropes.description: The JOKER 9.1MM rope is a single, double and twin rope that can be adapted to many different climbing situations. It offers the right balance between lightness, fluidity and safety to please experienced climbers and will also suit, thanks to its versatility, more traditional climbers looking for a rope that can be used
pros: Low weight per meter reduces rope drag on long pitches
Gentle on the body and gear during falls
Unicore construction keeps sheath and core locked together
cons: Not much variety in the color range

ropes.description: The JOKER 9.1MM rope is a single, double and twin rope that can be adapted to many different climbing situations. It offers the right balance between lightness, fluidity and safety to please experienced climbers and will also suit, thanks to its versatility, more traditional climbers looking for a rope that can be used
pros: Low impact force for softer catches
Good middle ground on handling feel
Unicore construction keeps sheath and core locked together
cons: Thin diameter trades durability for weight savings

ropes.description: Beal's intermediate cragging rope offering the perfect compromise between handling and durability. With 7 UIAA falls and a low 7.5 kN impact force, the Karma suits progressive climbers stepping up from beginner ropes. Made with Bluesign-certified nylon.
pros: Good balance of weight and durability
Low impact force makes falls less jarring
cons: Heavier rope to haul on big walls

ropes.description: With its weight of 48 grams per meter, the OPERA 8.5 is the lightest and thinnest single rope on the market. The first to weigh less than 50 grams. A true concentrate of all BÉAL's know-how, it combines several technological innovations to offer a resistant rope that is both ultra-light and ultra-fluid. With its low im
pros: Sheath repels water on wet rock and ice
Unicore construction keeps sheath and core locked together
Triple certification gives maximum flexibility in the mountains
cons: Harder to grip and catch on belay with thin gloves

ropes.description: With its weight of 48 grams per meter, the OPERA 8.5 is the lightest and thinnest single rope on the market. The first to weigh less than 50 grams. A true concentrate of all BÉAL's know-how, it combines several technological innovations to offer a resistant rope that is both ultra-light and ultra-fluid. With its low im
pros: Soft catch reduces strain on protection
cons: Not compatible with most assisted braking devices

ropes.description: Beal's Rando 8.0 is a ultralight half rope designed for alpine hiking and mountaineering expeditions. Limited to 20-30m lengths, it provides critical redundancy for technical alpine terrain.
pros: Soft and flexible in the hand
cons: Low UIAA fall count limits lifespan

ropes.description: Versatile, light and fluid, this 9.4MM rope offers an intermediate diameter for thin ropes. The latter has been reinforced by our UNICORE technology providing resistance to friction and increased safety. A model designed for high-level climbing but also suitable for mountaineering.
pros: Low impact force (7.8 kN)  -  soft catches
Good balance of weight and durability
Low impact force makes falls less jarring
Unicore construction keeps sheath and core locked together

ropes.description: Versatile, light and fluid, this 9.4MM rope offers an intermediate diameter in the thin ropes range. This rope has been reinforced with our UNICORE technology, which provides resistance to friction and increased safety. A model designed for high level climbing but also adapted to mountaineering.
pros: Holds its weight and handling feel in wet weather
Trusted construction from a well-known manufacturer
cons: Low UIAA fall count limits lifespan

ropes.description: Made in France, the SUNRISE single rope and the SUNUP double rope are the first ropes in the world to be 100% recycled and have a very low carbon impact.
cons: Won't survive as many falls as thicker ropes

ropes.description: Beal's The One 9.6 is a versatile all-purpose single rope designed for gym and sport climbing. Oeko-Tex certified for environmental responsibility without sacrificing performance.
pros: Made by a company with decades of rope-making experience
cons: Heavier rope to haul on big walls

ropes.description: The TIGER 10MM is an ideal rope in terms of versatility. In climbing, it is the first choice rope for climbers starting using thin ropes. It is characterized by its ease of handling and its ease of clipping. It is good to know that it also has a diameter suitable for all self-locking belay devices. The TIGER 10MM is no
pros: Lowest impact force in class (7.6 kN)
Low impact force for softer catches
Doesn't gain weight from moisture like untreated ropes
cons: Basic feature set without standout innovations

ropes.description: The TIGER 10MM is an ideal rope in terms of versatility. In climbing, it is the first choice rope for climbers starting using thin ropes. It is characterized by its ease of handling and its ease of clipping. It is good to know that it also has a diameter suitable for all self-locking belay devices. The TIGER 10MM is no
pros: Water resistance extends the rope's usable life
Bonded core means the sheath won't bunch up on a cut
Comes from a brand with proven quality control

ropes.model: Top Gun II 10.5 Unicore Dry Cover
ropes.description: This high performance rope with its low impact force (7.4kN) and the record number of falls (11) it can withstand, already offered us excellent results and an optimal level of safety for peace of mind when climbing It is now reinforced by our UNICORE innovative technology which provides undeniable additional safety. Th
pros: Handles a lot of whippers before retirement
Sheath repels water on wet rock and ice

ropes.model: Top Gun II 10.5 Unicore Golden Dry
ropes.description: This high performance rope with its low impact force (7.4kN) and the record number of falls (10) it can withstand, already offered us excellent results and an optimal level of safety for peace of mind when climbing It is now reinforced by our UNICORE innovative technology which provides undeniable additional safety. Th
pros: Built to take repeated falls without degrading quickly
Soft catches that are easier on the climber and gear
Water rolls off the treated outer layer
Bonded core means the sheath won't bunch up on a cut
cons: Thick diameter adds bulk to the pack

ropes.description: The VIRUS 10MM is a rope that is characterized by its extreme versatility. It works with all types of equipment and in all circumstances. Moreover, its balanced diameter guarantees increased control and durability over time. A model of maneuverability and safety to take full advantage of your climbs, particularly appre
pros: Low impact force for softer catches
Treatment blocks moisture and grit from getting into the core
cons: Too thick for advanced outdoor climbing
If the sheath gets damaged, it can slide independently

ropes.description: Beal's Wall Cruiser 9.6 Unicore bridges gym and crag climbing. The Unicore construction provides enhanced durability and handling, with Golden Dry treatment for all-weather performance.
pros: Survives more falls than most ropes in its class
Soft and flexible in the hand
cons: Impact force on the high side for its class

ropes.description: Purpose-built for climbing walls and gyms. The thick 10.5mm diameter with 47% sheath and Unicore bonding provides maximum abrasion resistance for top-roping, auto-belays, and heavy daily gym use. Available in shorter lengths optimized for indoor walls.
pros: Moisture resistance means it stays manageable in the mountains

ropes.description: The ZENITH 9.5 MM is a single rope characterized by its lightness and manageability. Its diameter gives it greater fluidity in the carabiner, which makes it easier to clip. Balanced between compactness, lightness and handling, it is ideal for performance on crags.
pros: Dry sheath keeps the rope performing in rain and snow

ropes.description: One of the thinnest twin ropes available at just 7.0mm and 36 g/m. The BD 7.0 Dry is designed for technical alpine climbing where two-rope redundancy is essential and every gram counts. Dry Performance treatment for wet and icy conditions.
pros: Survives more falls than most ropes in its class
cons: Twin only  -  must clip both ropes
Very thin  -  wears fast on sharp rock

ropes.description: A versatile half/twin rope for alpine and ice climbing. At just 48 g/m, the 8.5 Dry is built for demanding alpine routes where weight is critical. Dry Performance treatment keeps it performing in wet and icy conditions. Use as half ropes on wandering routes or twin for straightforward lines.
pros: Exceptionally high fall rating for long-term durability

ropes.description: Black Diamond's skinniest single rope, designed for demanding sport and trad rock climbs. The 9.4mm diameter delivers a supple feel ideal for long multi-pitches and wandering routes. Dry Performance treatment on core and sheath repels moisture effectively.
pros: Supple handling for easy clipping and belaying
cons: Thinner ropes wear faster on sharp rock
Basic feature set without standout innovations

ropes.description: A thick, durable workhorse rope built for top roping, group cragging, and heavy daily use. The 9.9mm diameter provides excellent durability and longevity, making it a great pick for climbers who prioritize rope life over weight savings.
pros: Sheath doesn't soak up moisture like untreated alternatives
cons: Too thick for demanding alpine routes
Not the best choice when weight matters

ropes.description: Black Diamond's 9.2 Dry is a lighter, more nimble dry rope for climbers seeking weight savings without sacrificing durability. Perfect for sport climbing and multipitch routes.
pros: Good UIAA fall count for its weight class
Pleasant to handle on long belay sessions
cons: May feel dated compared to newer constructions

ropes.description: BlueWater's perfect all-arounder combining performance with exceptional longevity. The 10.5mm Accelerator delivers high UIAA fall rating and very high durability for long-term use on varied climbing.
pros: Good UIAA fall count for its weight class
Balanced handling between supple and stiff
cons: Higher impact force makes falls harder on the body

ropes.description: BlueWater's workhorse all-round rope offering low impact force for a larger diameter. The 10.2mm Eliminator provides excellent durability and handling for sport and trad climbing with balanced performance.
pros: Good UIAA fall count for its weight class
Supple handling for easy clipping and belaying

ropes.description: BlueWater's heavy-duty 11mm rope for big wall and guiding applications. Exceptional 15 UIAA fall rating and heavy 77g/m weight provide maximum durability and rope longevity for demanding climbing.
pros: Exceptionally high fall rating for long-term durability
Sheath repels water on wet rock and ice

ropes.description: BlueWater's lightweight performance single rope with a unique 40-carrier sheath for a tighter weave despite the thin diameter. The Icon 9.1 is the go-to rope for Tommy Caldwell and Cedar Wright. Light enough for long approaches while still offering 35% sheath mass for durability.
pros: Good value for a skinny rope
Water rolls off the treated outer layer
cons: Can be stiff and prone to kinking
Low UIAA fall count limits lifespan
Higher impact force makes falls harder on the body

ropes.description: A versatile American-made 9.7mm single rope excelling in sport, trad, and ice climbing. The Lightning Pro boasts 8 UIAA falls with a remarkably low 7.8 kN impact force — among the softest catches in its class. Double Dry treatment keeps it performing in wet conditions.
pros: Good UIAA fall count for its weight class
cons: No bonding technology between core and outer layer

ropes.description: BlueWater's mid-range single 10mm rope in eye-catching neon colors for excellent visibility. Offers balanced performance for sport and trad climbing with reliable dry treatment.
pros: Good UIAA fall count for its weight class
Supple handling for easy clipping and belaying
cons: Neon colors may not appeal to all
Heavier pull through alpine terrain with ledges and corners

ropes.description: BlueWater's lightweight advanced rope for sport climbers seeking a thin, responsive feel. The 9.2mm Xenon combines low weight (55g/m) with reliable handling and dry treatment for wet conditions.
pros: Pleasant to handle on long belay sessions
Moisture resistance means it stays manageable in the mountains
cons: Fewer color choices than some competitors

ropes.description: Edelrid's ultralight half rope for serious alpine objectives. Triple-rated with 9 UIAA falls (as half), it's one of the most capable thin ropes on the market. Pro Dry and partially recycled.
pros: Reliable durability under normal lead climbing use
cons: Thin diameter requires compatible belay devices

ropes.description: The world's first climbing rope made from renewable bio-based materials. Over 40% of the Birdlime is made from castor-oil-based material, reducing fossil fuel dependency. Thermo Shield treatment ensures excellent handling. The '1R' designation means 'Reduce' in Edelrid's sustainability system.
pros: Supple handling for easy clipping and belaying

ropes.description: The thinner sibling of the Boa Eco 9.8, now at 9.6mm for improved handling. Made from high-quality leftover yarns from Edelrid's own production — each rope looks unique. The '2R' designation means 'Reuse' in Edelrid's sustainability naming. Excellent all-rounder at a budget price.
pros: Sheath repels water on wet rock and ice
cons: Each rope looks different  -  no color consistency
Less choice on appearance than other brands

ropes.description: The greenest rope in the lineup — made entirely from recycled materials with Bluesign certification and no PFC treatment. The 9.8mm diameter and 9 UIAA falls make it a bombproof gym and single-pitch workhorse. Available in a short 40m gym length. The perfect first rope.
pros: Balanced handling between supple and stiff
cons: Impact force on the high side for its class

ropes.description: One of the thinnest and lightest single ropes available, also triple-rated for use as a half or twin rope. At 51 g/m the Canary Pro Dry is a specialist tool for alpine climbing and ice routes where every gram matters. Pro Dry treatment meets UIAA water repellent standard.
pros: Pro Dry meets UIAA water standard
47% sheath for durability despite thin diameter
Relaxed feel that knots and uncoils without fuss
cons: Only 5 UIAA falls as single rope
Replace sooner due to fewer rated falls

ropes.description: Edelrid's high-fall-count sport rope. With 9 UIAA falls it's one of the most durable 9.5mm ropes on the market. Pro Dry treatment, Bluesign certified, and partially made from recycled materials.
pros: Solid fall rating for regular sport and trad use

ropes.description: The most robust single rope in Edelrid's lineup. Aramid fibers woven into the sheath provide outstanding cut resistance for sharp rock and big wall climbing. 48-bobbin construction creates a compact, fine sheath. Pro Dry treatment meets UIAA water repellent standard.
pros: Reliable durability under normal lead climbing use
Cut-resistant aramid fibers in the sheath
cons: Heavier at 63 g/m due to aramid
Higher impact force makes falls harder on the body

ropes.description: The world's first rope made from 100% recycled materials — both core and sheath. The NEO 100 3R closes the materials cycle using Edelrid's in-house factory waste from Isny, Germany. Combines mechanically and chemically recycled nylon for zero-compromise performance. Lowest carbon footprint of any Edelrid single rope.
Edelrid's Neo 3R 9.8 is made with recycled materials for environmentally conscious climbers. Maintains excellent performance while reducing environmental impact.
pros: Fall rating holds up well for the thickness
Relaxed feel that knots and uncoils without fuss
cons: Impact force on the high side for its class

ropes.description: Edelrid's EN 1891 Type A static rope for rappelling, hauling, and rope access. The 10.5mm diameter provides excellent abrasion resistance with 45% sheath and 31kN breaking strength. Available in long cuts up to 200m for fixed line setups. NOT for lead climbing — no dynamic fall absorption.

Handles like a well-broken-in rope from day one

ropes.description: Czech-made all-rounder with TeFix sheath technology for consistent handling over the rope's lifetime. Complete Shield dry treatment covers core and sheath. Excellent value for a fully treated 9.4mm single rope. A strong alternative to the big Western European brands.
pros: Solid fall rating for regular sport and trad use

ropes.description: Tendon's Master Pro 9.2 Complete Shield combines aramid protection with Complete Shield technology for maximum durability. Ideal for sharp rock and demanding trad climbing.
pros: Solid fall rating for regular sport and trad use
Stays cleaner and drier than ropes without treatment

ropes.description: Tendon's budget workhorse rope — durable, reliable, and incredibly affordable. The Smart 10.0 offers 8 UIAA falls and low 5.7% static elongation at the lowest price point in its class. Perfect as a first rope or dedicated gym/top-rope cord.
pros: Reliable durability under normal lead climbing use
cons: Heavier rope to haul on big walls

ropes.description: Tendon's budget-friendly all-rounder. The Smart Lite 9.8 is just as durable as any Tendon rope but at a fantastically low price — making it a great first rope for new outdoor climbers. Czech-made quality at an entry-level price.
pros: Light for its class at 62 g/m
Water resistance extends the rope's usable life
cons: Impact force on the high side for its class
No bonding technology between core and outer layer

ropes.description: Budget-friendly 10mm static rope for caving and fixed-line.
pros: Low stretch for precise rappel and rescue work
Reliable for top-rope setups and fixed lines
Outer coating protects against dirt and dampness
cons: Never use for lead climbing - zero dynamic elongation

ropes.description: Durable 11mm static rope for demanding applications.
pros: High breaking strength for fixed-line and hauling
Low stretch for precise rappel and rescue work
Comes from a brand with proven quality control
cons: Never use for lead climbing - zero dynamic elongation

ropes.description: Tendon's Trust 11.0 is a gym-focused thick rope with exceptional durability. The 11mm diameter provides optimal handling for indoor climbing and high-volume gym use.
pros: Survives more falls than most ropes in its class
cons: Heavier pull through alpine terrain with ledges and corners

ropes.description: Triple-rated for single, double, or twin use with an impressive 9 UIAA fall count. The Red Flag treatment marks the final five meters at each end with bright red dye for end-of-rope awareness. One of the most versatile ropes on the market at just 56 g/m.
pros: 9 UIAA falls  -  excellent durability for diameter
Fall rating well above average for the diameter
cons: Higher impact force than some competitors

ropes.description: Trango's versatile 9.8mm workhorse with excellent dry treatment and great value. High 13 UIAA fall rating combined with supple handling makes it an outstanding all-rounder for climbers seeking reliable performance without premium pricing.
pros: High UIAA fall count extends the rope's working life
Soft enough to feed through devices without resistance
